title: hymenophyllum grandis
intro: Hymenophyllum grandis, also known as the grand fern or the sensitive fern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a type of epiphytic fern that is native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its delicate and lacy fronds. The medicinal properties of Hymenophyllum grandis are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture.
